content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 207 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95314

October 8, 1976

John Harlow
Emily L. Harlow
3 Malibu Cove Colony Drive
Malibu, California 90265

Dear Mr. and Mrs. Harlow:

Thank you for your letter dated September 30, 1976, which was received by the Fair Political Practices Commission. Your letter asked whether the Malibu Committee for Incorporation would be required to file a financial report disclosing contributions and expenditures received and made prior to June 10, 1976, the date the Los Angeles Board of Supervisors approved the petition for incorporation and ordered that the petition be placed on the November 2, 1976, ballot.

Based on the Commission's holding in the Opinion requested by Mark Fontana, 2 FPPC Opinions 25 (No. 75-162, February 4, 1976), the Malibu Committee for Incorporation would not have to report activity prior to June 10, 1976. As the conclusion in the Fontana Opinion, which also concerned a proposal for incorporation, stated:

The incorporation proposal does not become a "measure" within the meaning of Government Code Section 82043 until the Board of Supervisors of Santa Barbara County orders the proposal placed on the ballot. Consequently, IVCC's reporting obligations relative to contributions received and expenditures made in support of the incorporation proposal do not commence until after that action by the Board of Supervisors.... [Emphasis added]

The only exception to the statement that there would be no reporting obligation prior to June 10, 1976, is explained in footnote 5 of the Fontana Opinion, 2 FPPC Opinions at 28.


John Harlow
Emily L. Harlow
Page Two
October 8, 1976


According to that footnote, the Committee for the Incorporation of Malibu would have to report if it received contributions and made expenditures "in anticipation of the measure being placed on the ballot, even if such contributions and expenditures were made before the Board of Supervisors actually placed the proposal on the ballot."

For your information, I am enclosing a copy of the Fontana Opinion. I hope this information is helpful to you. If you have any other questions, please contact Barbara Campbell, an attorney in the Enforcement Division.

Sincerely,

Kenneth Finney
Chief
Legal Division

KF:BC:jo

Enclosure

cc: George Vertalney


September 30, 1976

Daniel H. Lowenstein, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Mr. Chairman:

The attached data is being submitted to you by us, as private citizens. We are requesting an investigation as to whether the Malibu Committee for Incorporation represented by Mr. Jerry Lewi and Mr. Vic Gainer (addresses on letterhead attached) are required to file a financial report of funds collected by their organization prior to June 10, 1976.

This question is raised now as they continue to be a Bona-Fide organization and submitted their Committee's ballot argument in support of incorporation which has been selected for the sample ballot by the Los Angeles County Registrar-Recorder, Mr. Leonard Panish.

To our knowledge, no funds have been collected by this organization since June 10, 1976, when the Los Angeles Board of Supervisors approved the petition for incorporation and ordered that an election be called for November 2, 1976.

Based on relevant information contained in FPPC opinion No. 75-162 dated February 4, 1976, we are asking your office to determine if Mr. Gainer and Mr. Lewi would be required to file a financial report, and if so, notification be sent to them.

This matter has been discussed with Mr. George Vertelney, FPPC of the Los Angeles Area, and has resulted in this request from your office.

It would be appreciated if you would please send all copies of correspondence pertaining to this matter. Our home address is below.

Sincerely yours,

John Harlow and Emily L. Harlow
3 Malibu Cove Colony Drive
Malibu, California 90265
Res: (213) 457-7847
Office: (213) 391-0711 X-7101

Attachments

cc: George Vertelney, FPPC
    Los Angeles County, Registrar-Recorder
    808 North Spring Street
    Los Angeles, California 90012


JERRY LEWI, CO-CHAIRMAN
28860 Selfridge Drive
Malibu, California 90265
(213) 457-7167

VIC GAINER, CO-CHAIRMAN
20312 Pacific Coast Hwy.
Malibu, California 90265
(213) 456-8757

September 7, 1976

Mr. Don Dailey
Office of the Registrar of Voters
800 No. Spring Street
Los Angeles, California

RE: Malibu Incorporation Proponent
Ballot Statement Submitted
September 2, 1976

Dear Mr. Dailey,

In response to your request, we are furnishing you with
completed and signed copies of two form statements.

In addition, we offer a brief account of the history of
our committee's involvement with respect to this ballot measure.

The Malibu Committee on Incorporation was formed in November of 1973 for the purpose of studying the feasibility of incorporation for Malibu.

In April of 1975 we changed our name to the Malibu Committee
for Incorporation and subsequently filed our application with
LAFCO. (Copy of cover sheet attached). On December 10, 1975
we made our presentation at a LAFCO hearing and received their
approval to circulate a legal petition.

On March 25, 1975 the Board of Supervisors was notified that
we had complied with this step. (Attached copy of letter from
James Mize).

On June 10, 1976 the Board of Supervisors conducted a boundary hearing, which resulted in an election resolution. (Copy attached).

-1-


Page 2

On June 23, we received a copy of the impartial analysis
(attached) and were apprised that a proponent statement should
be prepared. We have done so, and submitted it to you.

Mr. Levi and I have been the only co-chairmen of our committee
since its inception. At no time during our committee's existence
have we sought association status. Our steering committee has
varied in size; people have come and gone...Several are now can-
didates for City Council. For these and other reasons, we felt
it best not to collect or spend money after the adoption of an
election resolution. We have reviewed our status with John Green-
wood of the FPPC who indicated that we may author the proponent
statement without jeopardizing our reporting status.

We respectfully offer all of this to assist you in determining
the most representative proponent group, and appreciate your con-
sideration of this matter.

Sincerely,

Vic Geiner
Co-Chairman,
Malibu Committee for Incorporation

VG:so
cc: Jerry Levi

[illegible]
[illegible]
CHICAGO POLICE DEPARTMENT
355 SOUTH STATE STREET
[illegible] IL 60604


JERRY LUWI, CO-CHAIRMAN
26230 Selfridge Drive
Malibu, California 90265
(213) 457-7167

SEP 2 9 1976
MALIBU CO-CHAIRMAN
RECEIVED Pacific Coast Hwy.
Malibu, California 90265
(213) 456-8757

ARGUMENT FOR THE PROPOSED INCORPORATION
OF THE CITY OF MALIBU

Incorporation is the only sure way for Malibu residents to
control Malibu's growth and to preserve Malibu's semi-rural life
style. Incorporation provides the best possible protection against
overdevelopment.


BENEFITS & OBJECTIVES:

1. Develop a general plan to preserve Malibu's semi-rural
   character.

2. Prevent more high density developments and their
   consequences: Congestion on the Pacific Coast Highway,
   air pollution, and higher taxes to pay for increased
   urban services.

3. Local control of local taxes by a locally elected
   resident city council, responsive to the community's
   needs.

4. Provide the community services at a level desired by
   the resident tax-paying voters.

5. Prevent increased county taxes in an unincorporated
   Malibu under the Alatorre Law.


COST ANALYSIS:

The estimated city tax would be 31 cents per $100 of assessed
valuation - about $1.00 per week for the average home in Malibu.


JERRY LEWI, CO-CHAIRMAN
28260 Selfridge Drive
Malibu, California 90265
(213) 457-7167

VIC GAINER, CO-CHAIRMAN
20832 Pacific Coast Hwy.
Malibu, California 90265
(213) 456-8757

The proposed city budget compares favorably with other

contract cities of similar size and character and has been

thoroughly reviewed for validity and accuracy by:

Urban Development Group, an independent consulting firm.
The Local Agency Formation Commission

Cost analyses of other cities indicates that a large

industrial and commercial tax base is unnecessary to maintain

a balanced budget.


THE ALTERNATIVES TO INCORPORATION:

The continuation of the present county policies would:

1. Urbanize Malibu, turning this rural, recreational
community of primarily single family homes into an
unbroken urbanized sprawl of condominiums, apartments,
and high density housing.

2. Lead to increased taxes to provide services for the
urbanization of Malibu.

3. Create pressure for an area-wide sewer system.

4. Destroy the scenic beauty of the hills, canyons, and
beaches.

5. Add more traffic to the already overburdened Pacific
Coast Highway, creating renewed pressure for a freeway
through Malibu.

SOLUTION: ONLY LOCAL CONTROL WILL PREVENT THESE THINGS FROM HAPPENIN

We urge you to vote FOR incorporation.

Vic Gainer, Jerry Lewis, Co-chairmen
Malibu Committee For Incorporation


Since the document is essentially blank except for possibly a stray mark, there is no substantial text to transcribe.

BONA FIDE ASSOCIATION OF CITIZENS FILING ARGUMENTS
FOR OR AGAINST [illegible] MEASURES

1. Name of Association: MAUI/SA COMMUNITY for INCORPORATION

2. Fictitious Name, if any:_________________________________

3. Address and Telephone Number of Association: 26542 PACIFIC
   COAST HWY., MALIBU, CALIF. 90265 (213)456-3757

4. When was Association formed? APRIL 1975

5. Name, address, office held and telephone number during business
   hours of Officers.

        Name & Office                    Address & Phone
        VIC GOWDEN, CHAIRMAN            2634 PACIFIC COAST HWY    213-456-3757(H)
        SARAH LEVI, CO-CHAIRMAN         28360 SEAFLOGE RD.        653-2279(OFFICE)

   _________________________________  _________________________________

6. Does Association have a Charter, By-Laws or other Articles of
   Association? Yes_____ No 2.

7. How many members? 1170 - 1122

8. Meetings held: Weekly__/ Monthly_____ Other AS NEEDED_____

9. Brief description of activities of Association: STUDY ECONOMIC
   FEASIBILITY OF MALIBU INCORPORATION PRESENT FINDINGS TO
   LAFCO BOARD & COMMUNITY. FROM APRIL OF 1975

10. Are dues collected? Yes_____ No_____  TO JUL OF 1976 THE ONLY PROJECT
                                         ORGANIZATION.


§ 3.2.

S.3720. Amendments concerning measures filed pursuant to this division shall be
Argument; form statement

[illegible] Assessments concerning measures filed pursuant to this division shall be
supported by the following form statement, to be signed by each author of the
argument:

The undersigned author(s) of the _________________ argument
(Motion of _____________, [illegible])
is true and correct in the best of [illegible]

Signed _________________ Knowledge and belief.

(Added by [illegible] [illegible].)
Date Sept. 7, 1974

§ 3352. Clarification of signatures

The clerk may use copies of the affidavits of registration for the purpose of
checking signatures on petitions.
(Added by Resolution, § 359, P. ___; § 1, [illegible], eff. Sept. 13, 1974.)

§ C.C


Sep 10 || 3 || E-75
CORRECTED AND
[illegible]


Clerk of the Board of Supervisors
323 Hall of Administration
Los Angeles, California 90012

Local Agency Formation Commission
Registrar-Recorder

At its meeting held June 10, 1976, the Board took the following action

4

Notice having been duly given, hearing was held at the time and
place regularly set, on the petition for the proposed incorporation
of a general law city to be known as the City of Malibu.

Interested persons addressed the Board. Written requests for
exclusions were presented.

On motion of Supervisor Hayes, seconded by Supervisor Ward,
unanimously carried (Supervisor Hahn being absent), the hearing was
closed.

Supervisor Hayes made a motion to exclude Sunset Mesa and Los Angeles
Athletic Club from boundaries of incorporation. Said motion failed
to carry for lack of a second.

On motion of Supervisor Hayes, seconded by Supervisor Edelman,
unanimously carried (Supervisor Hahn being absent), the Board denied
all requests for exclusion and set the boundaries as approved by
the Local Agency Formation Commission on December 10, 1975 and
determined that 8,268 registered voters reside within the area of
said proposed incorporation as of the last General State Election.

It was further ordered that an election be called to be consolidated
with the General Election on November 2, 1976, for the purpose of
determining whether said territory, as a general law city to be known
as the City of Malibu and for the purpose of electing officers, the
precincts, polling places and other officers for said incorporation elect
shall be those designated and appointed for the General Election.

The Registrar-Recorder was instructed to conduct the canvass of
votes of said election and the Executive Officer was instructed to
give notice of election as required by law. The Local Agency
Formation Commission was requested to develop an impartial analysis
for the Ballot Proposition.

The boundaries are as follows:

Copies distributed:
Each Supervisor
Chief Administrative Officer
County Counsel
Auditor-Controller
Road Commissioner
Assessor

Mr. J. J. Bentley
Mr. J. Lewi
Mr. Vic Gainer
Mr. Peter Ostroff
